other_observers: none; A bird matching this description was reported earlier in the day at the same location by William Kaempfer and John Vanderpoel. (I believe they reported the gull on ebird and Colorado Bird News as glaucous-winged rather than hybrid.)  Through a detailed photo comparison (looking at unique color patterns on individual feathers), I have determined that this glaucous-winged x herring gull is the same one reported by Paul Hurtado at Pueblo Reservoir on 12/29/2012 (http://ebird.org/ebird/v
specific_location: Teller Lake No. 5 40.039 deg N, 105.137 deg W
description: I am reporting this bird as GLAUCOUS-WINGED x HERRING GULL, not pure glaucous-winged.  The bird was large -- similar in length to nearby herring gulls. It had a bulky body; large, flat-topped head; and large, mostly dark bill. Plumage was pale overall with darker patterning. The darkest color in the folded primaries was similar to the darkest color in the checkered pattern on the main body. The colors captured by my camera vary among the photos; I would consider the photo with the glaucous-winged x herring gull in front of a herring gull most representative of the colors my eyes saw.  The indicators that the bird was a hybrid with herring gull include (1) the prominent patterning on the wings and (2) patches of somewhat lighter color in the mostly-black bill.Behaviors: The bird was feeding on dead fish along with about 150 other gulls (ring-billed, herring, and Thayer's).Call: unknownPlumage: first winter
photographer: Photographer: David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell
